Re: [.ate Contribution Reports
Dear Ms. Hughes,
The purpose of this letter is to confirm our telephone conversation of
September 11, 199L.
Stan Oken participated in a special election for Fresno County Supervisor,
District 5, on August 13, 1991. On July 31, 1991, and August 2, 1991, the
candidate made loans to the campaigrr of $10,000 on each date. At that
time we understood the 24 hour reporting requirement to only apply to
contributions received from third parties.
On August 6, L991, we received money from third parties as well as another
loan from the candidate. Upon looking up the requirements for reporting
the receipt of money from the third parties we realized that we had
misinterpreted the guidelines and that the candidate's loans were required to
be reported as well. We immediately called the Fresno County Elections
Department to inquire how to correct the problem and filed the [:te
Contribution Reports that same day, August 6, 1991. The Elections
Department issued a waiver of the penalty fee at that time.
We have since then confirmed with the Fresno County Elections Department
that we have met the requirement of the law regarding these reports and
there is nothing further we are required to do at this time. We do
understand that the loans must be reported on the next Form 490 to be
filed.
FRIENDS TO ELECT STAN OKEN
677 Scott Ave. o Clovis, CA 93612 o Bob Prtce, Treasurer
,<F"


Itds. Alice Hughes
Fair Political Practices Commission
September 11, 1991
Page Two
Pleasc confirm to us in writing that you concur that this letter is an acrurate
summary of our telephone conversation.
Thank you for your cooperation.
Sincerely,
d, A cilk
Gary A Colbert
Co-Treasurer
